steps: Go to https://cloud.arduino.cc/home/api-keys and sign in to your Arduino account (or create one at https://login.arduino.cc/)
Enter a name for your API key and click 'Continue'
Copy and save the Client ID and Client Secret shown — the secret will not be shown again

To get an access token, make a POST request to https://api2.arduino.cc/iot/v1/clients/token with body: grant_type=client_credentials, client_id=YOUR_ID, client_secret=YOUR_SECRET, audience=https://api2.arduino.cc/iot
Use the returned access token as a Bearer token in subsequent API calls

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: The OAuth2 Client ID from the Arduino IoT Cloud API Keys page

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: The OAuth2 Client Secret from the Arduino IoT Cloud API Keys page. Shown only once at creation.

notes: Uses OAuth2 Client Credentials flow — no user login required, suitable for server-to-server use
Client Secret is shown only once at creation — save it securely before closing the dialog
Access tokens expire and must be refreshed using the same client credentials
Free plan available with limited device and data storage quota

test_endpoint.description: List all IoT Things in your account (requires valid Bearer access token)
